MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Text Ruling

Motion To Remove Mechanics Lien
Off Calendar Jul-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
James Barrett, Esq. 650-969-3687 for Plaintiff.
Daniel Oren, Esq. 415-984-8237 Nixon Peabody for Defendant.

Argued. Mr. Oren contests the tentative ruling. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adpts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TAYLOR LEMBI Motion To Remove Mechanics Lien.

Defendant Taylor Lembi's motion to remove mechanic's lien is denied.

"The mechanics' lien law is mandated by the California Constitution. Due to this unique constitutional command, 'the courts have uniformly classified the mechanics' lien laws as remedial legislation, to be liberally construed for the protection of laborers and materialmen.' Nevertheless, this liberal construction rule may not be applied to frustrate the Legislature's manifested intent to exact strict compliance with the preliminary notice requirement." (Shady Tree Farms, LLC v. Omni Financial, LLC (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 131, 135 (citations omitted)/

Defendant argues that the lien is unenforceable because plaintiff failed to serve the notice of lien with the documentation required by Civil Code section 8118(b) [proof that notice was given]. Defendant further argues the "Proof of Service of Affidavit" that was attached to the notice of lien was perjurious and defective. (Civil Code section 8118(a)(2).) The proof of service was dated on September 12, 2022, but attests to service that occurred in the future on September 14, 2022.

Defendant's arguments lack merit. The undisputed evidence shows that the defendant was served with the notice of lien. (Mashal Decl., pars. 1-7; Complaint Ex. 3.) Defendant cites no authority holding that technical errors in the proof of service renders the statutorily compliant notice defective. The law is to the contrary. "We conclude from the relevant authorities that, while the principle of strict construction applies to 'the manner or form of serving notice upon an affected party', it does not extend to matters of proof in a case where the defendant has admitted that notice was served in the statutorily prescribed manner." (Hub Construction Specialties, Inc. v. Esperanza Charities, Inc. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 855, 856-857 [mechanic's lien was not invalid where the plaintiff subcontractor failed to comply with a former statutory requirement of proof that the preliminary notice was served on the defendant property owner by certified mail] (citation omitted)).

There is also authority holding that even failure to comply with the notice requirement itself may not render the lien unenforceable where there is no prejudice. (Industrial Asphalt, Inc. v. Garrett Corp. (1986) 180 Cal.App.3d 1001[mechanic's lien enforceable even though the plaintiff only served the subcontractor and not the bankrupt contractor]; Wand Corp. v. San Gabriel Valley Lumber Co. (1965) 236 Cal.App.2d 855, 861 [where "no one is prejudiced, technical requirements shall not stand in the way of achieving the purpose of the Mechanics Lien Law"].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
